label: AXIS — tracker failed-stow modeled loss
source_denominator_or_scope: Per-MW modeled solar loss under a failed tracker-stow scenario; AXIS reference model
useful_for: upper stow-scenario severity anchor for tracker mounting/module failure
caveats: HAIL-STOW framed (stow to protect against HAIL); it is the tracker-stow failure severity, NOT isolated straight-line-wind DR. Modeled, not observed. Placed against MOUNTING+MODULE_ATTACH bucket only as an order-of-magnitude, with a grain-mismatch caveat.

label: AXIS — fixed-tilt modeled loss
source_denominator_or_scope: Per-MW modeled solar loss, fixed-tilt architecture
useful_for: fixed-tilt severity anchor (architecture split maps to v2 fixed_tilt pathway)
caveats: HAIL/NatCat-blended modeled figure, not isolated SLW. Fixed-tilt vs tracker split is the useful signal (maps to the v2 two-architecture design). Modeled, not observed.

label: AXIS — successful hail-stow modeled loss
source_denominator_or_scope: Per-MW modeled solar loss when hail stow succeeds
useful_for: lower stow-scenario severity anchor (mitigated case)
caveats: This is the SUCCESSFUL-HAIL-STOW residual, i.e. mostly a hail-mitigation figure, not straight-line wind. Shows the swing that stow behavior produces. Modeled.

label: AXIS — heat-strengthened glass upgrade cost
source_denominator_or_scope: Incremental module-glass upgrade cost per MW
useful_for: hardening-cost context vs TIV base
caveats: Mitigation COST, not a loss. Glass upgrade is HAIL hardening, not wind. Included as value-at-risk context only.

label: AXIS — modeled solar peril-cost share (Hail 55 / Wildfire 23 / Tornado 18)
source_denominator_or_scope: Peril-cost decomposition of a modeled solar portfolio loss
useful_for: peril-mix context; TORNADO carries 18% (straight-line wind not separately itemized)
caveats: Straight-line/convective wind is NOT a separate slice here — tornado is 18%, but derecho/microburst SLW is folded into other categories or unlisted. Peril share, not a $-severity. The companion AXIS statement 'hail 2nd most costly after strong winds' conflicts with this 55%-hail ordering, underscoring definitional ambiguity in 'wind'.

label: AXIS — $342M gross NatCat claim example
reported_unit: gross USD, single large NatCat solar claim
source_denominator_or_scope: Whole-event gross claim; MW capacity NOT stated
useful_for: order-of-magnitude ceiling for a large solar NatCat event (no MW denom)
caveats: No MW denominator -> $/MW normalization WITHHELD (blank, per golden rule). Peril mix within the $342M not disclosed (blended NatCat). Ceiling context only.

label: AXIS — 240-day NatCat downtime
source_denominator_or_scope: Downtime duration for a large NatCat solar event
useful_for: business-interruption duration context (separate metric family)
caveats: BI/downtime is a SEPARATE metric family from physical DR — keep off the physical-damage axis. Days, not $. No MW denominator.

label: GCube — hail average $58.4M/claim
reported_unit: avg gross USD per hail claim
source_denominator_or_scope: Average solar hail claim; whole-plant, MW not stated
useful_for: hail severity context; frames wind as the FREQUENCY (not top-severity) driver
caveats: HAIL claim average, NOT wind. No MW denominator -> not normalizable. Included to show hail dominates COST while wind leads FREQUENCY per GCube. Body gated.

label: GCube — hail = 54% of solar claim cost
source_denominator_or_scope: Hail share of aggregate solar claim cost
useful_for: peril-mix: hail dominates cost; strong wind a leading frequency driver
caveats: Hail cost share, not wind. The 'wind is a leading cause of insured losses' statement is a FREQUENCY claim, not a cost share. No wind $ isolated.

label: Descartes — tornado parametric limit $70M
source_denominator_or_scope: Parametric (index-triggered) tornado cover limit
useful_for: market tail-risk / capacity signal (tornado, parametric)
caveats: Parametric LIMIT, not an observed loss; TORNADO not straight-line wind. Structure signal only.

label: Descartes — worked payout $50M x 15% = $7.5M
source_denominator_or_scope: Worked example: $50M event x 15% payout ratio
useful_for: illustration of parametric payout mechanics (not an asset loss)
caveats: ILLUSTRATIVE parametric payout, NOT a ground-up asset loss and NOT straight-line wind. No MW denominator. Do not treat as severity.

label: Gallagher Re — 2025 SCS $60B / 47% of global insured losses
source_denominator_or_scope: Global all-property SCS industry total, 2025
useful_for: macro SCS scale context ONLY
caveats: GENERIC_ONLY: all-property, all-SCS-sub-peril global aggregate. NEVER divide by solar MW. Context only.

label: Verisk (AIR) — >70 damage functions incl. straight-line wind for solar codes 500-514/3001-3023
reported_unit: count of damage functions / occupancy codes
source_denominator_or_scope: Vendor cat model; solar-specific SLW damage function exists but ordinates licensed
useful_for: structural proof that straight-line wind is modeled as a DISTINCT solar damage function
caveats: The DR-vs-gust ordinates are gated -> no number to normalize. This row records the PATHWAY and the structural confirmation that SLW is isolable for solar. Not a benchmark value.

label: Moody's RMS — convective wind 'microbursts to derecho' renewable curves
source_denominator_or_scope: Vendor cat model; renewable (solar) convective-wind curves exist, ordinates licensed
useful_for: confirms the microburst->derecho SLW continuum is modeled for solar
caveats: Ordinates gated. PATHWAY + structural confirmation only, not a benchmark value.

label: Cedar Rapids 2020 derecho — ballasted array liftoff at ~140 mph vs 120 mph design
reported_value: array liftoff; ~140 mph vs ASCE 120 mph design
source_denominator_or_scope: 2020 derecho, Cedar Rapids IA; ballasted array; measured ~140 mph
useful_for: clean straight-line-wind/derecho FAILURE case above design; intensity anchor for the curve x-axis
caveats: Damage-state + intensity, NO dollar figure. Failure occurred ABOVE the 120 mph design (demand > capacity) — consistent with a threshold-logistic curve exceeding R0. Ballasted (not piled) foundation is a specific vulnerability.

label: Prescott AZ ~70 mph event destroyed an array (BELOW design)
source_denominator_or_scope: Prescott AZ; ~70 mph, below ASCE design
useful_for: SUB-DESIGN failure case (installation/anchorage defect, not demand>capacity)
caveats: Failure BELOW design gust -> attributable to installation/anchorage defect, NOT the design-demand curve. A caution that field DR can exceed the curve when workmanship is poor. No $.

label: DOE FEMP — repair/replace cost tiers $0.01-$1.50/W
source_denominator_or_scope: Per-watt repair/replace cost ladder by damage severity
useful_for: partial-loss $/W ladder to bound intermediate DR states
caveats: Cost tiers span 1% to ~134% of the $1.12/W TIV base ($10k-$1.5M/MW). NOT tied to a specific gust -> cannot place on the DR x-axis directly. Bounds the severity of partial-loss states, not a single benchmark.

label: FEMP tiers — DERIVED $/MW span (sensitivity)
useful_for: illustrative partial->total loss $/MW span for solar wind damage
caveats: DERIVED sensitivity, not a source-verbatim benchmark. The top of the range ($1.5M/MW = 134% TIV) exceeds installed TIV -> reflects removal+debris+replace, a GRAIN WARNING that repair cost can exceed asset TIV, not DR>1. normalized_usd_per_MW left BLANK.

label: Oakey (AU) — ~2,000 modules damaged at a 55 MW plant
source_denominator_or_scope: 55 MW plant, AU wind event; ~2,000 modules damaged
useful_for: module-attachment failure fraction anchor (module-attach FU)
caveats: Module COUNT, not $. At ~2,000 modules and typical ~2,700 modules/MW-ish packing the damaged fraction is small-single-digit % of one plant's modules; non-US climate; no gust ordinate. Partial-loss fraction context only.

label: Soltec — ~1% trackers collapsed + ~1% minor, perimeter rows
source_denominator_or_scope: Vendor wind event; ~2% of trackers affected, perimeter rows
useful_for: tracker-collapse fraction + perimeter spatial pattern (tracker-struct FU)
caveats: Vendor-authored; ~1-2% collapse FRACTION (not DR, not $). Perimeter-row concentration is a real aeroelastic mechanism. No gust ordinate. Fraction context only.

label: NEXTracker — 25-yr failure probability by stow (CA 1.7-7.4%, UT 2.7-14.6%, SA 0.1-1.0%)
source_denominator_or_scope: Vendor-modeled 25-yr cumulative tracker failure probability by stow strategy & site
useful_for: shows stow strategy swings failure PROBABILITY ~4-10x (modifiable risk)
caveats: PROBABILITY (25-yr cumulative), NOT single-event DR and NOT $. Vendor-modeled. SCREENING signal that stow behavior is a first-order modifiable driver, echoing the AXIS failed-vs-successful-stow $/MW gap.

label: CPP — torsional galloping onset <~40 m/s (~90 mph), flat stow raises Ucrit
source_denominator_or_scope: Single-axis tracker aeroelastic instability onset; system-specific
useful_for: the mechanism + intensity behind the v2 single-axis-tracker Ucrit axis
caveats: System-specific critical speed, NOT a fleet plug-in and NOT a loss. Intensity ordinate/mechanism for the tracker pathway. Flat (0-deg) stow is the key mitigation.

label: NREL — no elevated performance loss below ~56 mph (~90 km/h)
source_denominator_or_scope: PV fleet PLR vs wind exposure; degradation not catastrophic
useful_for: empirical lower threshold supporting the v1 R0 (DR~0 below design)
caveats: PERFORMANCE-degradation threshold, not catastrophic DR. Supports the threshold-logistic R0 (near-zero DR below design). ~1% median annual PLR is chronic degradation, a different metric family from event DR.

label: PVEL — tracker module load ±1800 Pa vs fixed-tilt ±2400 Pa
source_denominator_or_scope: Lab module mechanical load ratings by mounting type
useful_for: supports the fixed-tilt-vs-tracker design-pressure split in v2
caveats: Lab LOAD RATING (Pa), not a field DR or $. The tracker<fixed pressure gap (1800<2400 Pa) is why v2 splits the two architectures with different design-demand denominators.

label: PVEL — BOM-material MSS failure rate 7% (2024) -> 20% (2025)
source_denominator_or_scope: MSS test failure rate trend across module BOMs
useful_for: manufacturing-quality trend (mechanism), rising module vulnerability
caveats: LAB test failure rate, not field loss. Rising BOM failure is a manufacturing-quality signal that module-attach vulnerability may be increasing; not a $-severity.

label: USPVDB v4.0 — 131,573 MW-AC / ~162,802 MW-DC across 6,611 plants
source_denominator_or_scope: National utility-scale solar exposure base
useful_for: national exposure denominator for portfolio-share reasoning
caveats: Exposure count, NOT a loss. Illustrative national TIV ~$182B at $1,120,000/MW-DC is order-of-magnitude only. AC vs DC basis must be reconciled before dividing any numerator.

label: USPVDB — Plains/Midwest/TX convective corridor ~38.4% of tracked capacity (DERIVED)
reported_unit: % of national capacity in convective-wind corridor
source_denominator_or_scope: TX+Plains+Midwest superset; computed from USPVDB API 2026-07-13
useful_for: geographic-concentration argument: solar sits in the convective-wind belt
caveats: DERIVED task aggregate, NOT source-verbatim. Establishes that a large minority of US solar exposure is in the high straight-line-wind/derecho corridor. Exposure, not loss.

label: EIA-860 — 149,798.5 MW utility-scale solar (2025)
source_denominator_or_scope: Most-current national utility-scale solar capacity
useful_for: current-year exposure denominator
caveats: Exposure/denominator, not a loss. Net summer capacity basis differs from USPVDB AC/DC. TABLE-CROSSING RISK (wind vs solar columns) flagged in the source.

label: NREL/DOE Q1-2024 — utility-scale MMP $1.12/W-dc (= $1,120,000/MW-dc)
source_denominator_or_scope: 100 MW-dc reference system, Q1-2024
useful_for: THE installed-TIV cross-check; EXACT match to the dossier $1,120,000/MWdc base
caveats: Denominator/value basis, NOT a loss. Bottom-up model, pre-ITC, excludes land. Exact match validates the TIV base all normalization uses.

label: Damage_Modeling / solar value ladder — physical replaceable $877,796/MW-dc
source_denominator_or_scope: Upstream canonical solar value ladder (Summary sheet)
useful_for: THE physical-replaceable denominator for the failure-unit crosswalk
caveats: Denominator/value basis, NOT a loss. Physical replaceable (78.37% of TIV) excludes sunk/soft/non-physical (21.63%). Failure-unit value shares are measured against THIS base.
